title: Combustion Enthalpy and Standard Heat of Formation
content: The standard enthalpy of combustion ΔH°comb is the heat released when one mole of a substance is completely burned in O₂ at 25 °C, 1 atm. Measured in a bomb calorimeter: q_rxn = −(m·Cp_water + C_v_bomb)·ΔT. Hess's law: ΔH°f(compound) = Σ ΔH°comb(elements) − ΔH°comb(compound). Tabulated values serve as benchmarks for fuel energy content and thermochemical database construction.
